book_claims.claim: Listed as a calcium source (139 mg per cup cooked with moist heat, 14% DV) and omega-3 source (0.42 g EPA/DHA per 75 g cooked).
book_claims.mechanism: Shellfish source of calcium and long-chain omega-3 fatty acids; provides lean protein with anti-inflammatory EPA/DHA supporting cardiovascular longevity.

supplementary_research.finding: Shellfish-derived EPA and DHA reduce systemic inflammation, lower triglycerides, and are associated with reduced cardiovascular mortality; lobster's omega-3 content contributes meaningfully to weekly marine omega-3 intake. (PMID 22682084)
Lobster is a rich source of astaxanthin (a carotenoid antioxidant), selenium, and zinc — nutrients associated with reduced oxidative stress and immune aging. (PMID 29590458)

Shellfish including lobster provide high-quality complete protein with low saturated fat, plus selenium, zinc, copper, and omega-3 fatty acids; associated with reduced inflammatory aging markers in observational studies.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Marine omega-3s; phospholipid-bound form in shellfish is highly bioavailable
Bioavailability approximately 30%; contributes to daily calcium needs especially in low-dairy diets
Highly bioavailable from seafood; essential for glutathione peroxidase antioxidant system
Heme-associated zinc from shellfish is more bioavailable than plant-source zinc
Lobster is an unusually high copper source; important for cytochrome c oxidase and antioxidant enzymes
Complete amino acid profile; lower IGF-1 stimulation than red meat

synergies.description: Vitamin C from lemon enhances absorption of minerals from lobster and provides contrasting acidity; classic preparation combination
Anti-inflammatory anethole in fennel complements EPA/DHA in lobster for additive anti-inflammatory effect; traditional Mediterranean seafood pairing
Monounsaturated fats in olive oil enhance absorption of fat-soluble nutrients in lobster and reduce the pro-oxidative potential of polyunsaturated fatty acids
